join_order.notes: partkey max = n_parts (contiguous 1..2M), bitset ~250KB. MUST parallelize filter with OpenMP.
Use partsupp_pk_index dense_range, scan ~4 entries per partkey to match suppkey
Use orders_orderkey_lookup dense index → o_orderdate → extract year

filters.method: memmem on varlen_string offsets_plus_data, PARALLEL with OpenMP over row ranges

data_structures.part_filter: bitset using uint64_t[(n_parts/64)+1], partkeys are 1..n_parts (contiguous PK), NO extra scan for max_partkey needed — use n_parts directly
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local double[25][8] direct 2D array (nation × year_offset), year_offset = year - 1992

parallelism.notes: CRITICAL: filter_part MUST use #pragma omp parallel for over part rows (previous iter was single-threaded, wasting 81ms). Use thread-local bitset words with atomic OR, or partition bitset by partkey range. For main lineitem scan, use schedule(dynamic, 100000). All lineitem columns use MADV_SEQUENTIAL (not MADV_RANDOM) — access is at sequential index i with ~5% density gaps, readahead helps.

indexes.purpose: partsupp lookup by l_partkey, scan ~4 entries for matching l_suppkey

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category D (parallelism). filter_part consumed 81ms (47% of 172ms total) running single-threaded over 2M varlen strings. With 64 cores available, parallelizing this with OpenMP should reduce it to ~2ms. Secondary fix (Category C): remove unnecessary max_partkey scan (partkeys are contiguous 1..n_parts), and change lineitem column madvise from MADV_RANDOM to MADV_SEQUENTIAL — columns are accessed at sequential index i (with 5.48% qualifying density), so readahead is beneficial. These changes should bring total from 172ms to ~40-45ms.
